title: Airborne vs Droplet
script_summary: Explains the differences between airborne and droplet transmission scientifically.

segments.voiceover: Science has a clearer story about airborne vs droplet than social media does.
Airborne transmission involves tiny particles lingering in the air for hours. Think of aerosols.
Droplet transmission, on the other hand, is larger, heavier droplets that fall quickly—think sneezes and coughs.
Studies show airborne viruses can travel distances over 6 feet, while droplets typically travel less.
Understanding this helps us make better choices about masks and ventilation.
Stay informed! Like and follow for more science insights.
